reasoning: The market priced this at 0.26 on March 6. Although structural friction has emerged – most notably a heated early March Oval Office clash where Trump had to mediate between Musk and Secretary of State Marco Rubio – Trump has so far avoided publicly disparaging Musk. In fact, on March 11, Trump went out of his way to aggressively support Musk on Truth Social, explicitly announcing he would buy a Tesla to counter left-wing boycotts and defend Musk. This indicates that while internal tensions are rising (as often happens with Trump's associates), Trump is currently committed to maintaining a strong, unified public front with Musk. Because he actively defended Musk immediately after a major internal clash leaked, the near-term probability of public disparagement is slightly reduced, pulling my estimate down to 0.24.
